text:
In a verdant meadow under a moonlit night, little Luna the owl hooted softly. She'd hear things that werende't owls—cats purring, squirrels gossiping.

One thing she didn't get was why Mr. Fluffington would sleep soundly. Luna watched him paw the sleep out of his eyes. He liked mornings.

One morning, Luna woke up with an idea! Why didn't she have her own bed? She snuck into Mr. Fluffington's. The feathers startled him awake, but he just blinked. Well, he knew owls didn't sleep that much.

"Okay," he chuckled.

"You don't do that."

He blinked again. "I just know lots of... interesting facts."

"You know nothing." Luna hopped out and stretched. The sky was full.

"Want some?"

"No snacks."<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
